On this blessed occasion of your 25th Marriage Anniversary, we celebrate not just years, but a journey of nikah, love, patience, faith and companionship.

The exchange of this mala symbolizes a renewed promise — to walk together in sabr, shukr and mohabbat, seeking Allah’s pleasure in every step of life.

With the blessings of Gurudev Sri Sri Ravi Shankar Ji and heartfelt prayers from the All India Memon Jamaat Federation, we pray:

“May Allah fill your home with sakoon, your hearts with noor, and your bond with barakah. May you continue to be garments for one another, as mentioned in the Qur’an, and be a source of peace, strength and inspiration for many.”

Aameen. 🤲✨

1. Peace is the Essence of Islam

Islam teaches that true peace comes from living in harmony with:
	•	Allah (God)
	•	Other human beings
	•	Nature and creation

The daily greeting of Muslims is:

“As-salāmu ʿalaykum” – Peace be upon you
This shows that peace is not just a concept, but a daily practice.

⸻

2. Human Life is Sacred

Islam considers every human life sacred, regardless of religion, race, or nationality.

Qur’an 5:32

“Whoever kills a soul… it is as if he has killed all of humanity. And whoever saves a life, it is as if he has saved all of humanity.”

This verse is often quoted globally as one of the strongest foundations for universal peace.

⸻

3. Justice is the Foundation of Harmony

Islam teaches that peace without justice is incomplete.

Qur’an 16:90

“Indeed, Allah commands justice, excellence, and giving to relatives, and forbids immorality, wrongdoing, and oppression.”

Social harmony comes through:
	•	Fairness
	•	Protection of rights
	•	Standing against ظلم (oppression)

⸻

4. Respect for Other Faiths

Islam recognizes and honors other religions.

Qur’an 2:256

“There is no compulsion in religion.”

Qur’an 109:6

“For you is your religion, and for me is mine.”

This establishes religious freedom and coexistence as Islamic principles.

⸻

5. Kindness and Good Character

The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ said:

“The best among you are those with the best character.”
(Bukhari)

And:

“A Muslim is the one from whose tongue and hands people are safe.”
(Muslim)

Meaning: A true believer does not harm others—physically or verbally.

⸻

6. Forgiveness Over Revenge

Islam promotes forgiveness as a higher moral path.

Qur’an 41:34

“Repel evil with what is better, and the one between whom and you was hatred will become like a close friend.”

This is a powerful formula for transforming conflict into harmony.

⸻

7. Peace Even in Disagreement

The Prophet ﷺ never insulted, abused, or humiliated even his enemies. He prayed:

“O Allah, guide my people, for they do not know.”

At the conquest of Makkah, he forgave those who persecuted him for 13 years—this is considered one of the greatest examples of practical peace in history.

A Meeting of Hearts for Humanity

The meeting between Gurudev Sri Sri Ravi Shankar Ji and the All India Memon Jamaat Federation stands as a powerful symbol of peace, unity and shared human values.

Rooted in mutual respect, this اللقاء (mulaqat) reflects a collective commitment towards love beyond religion, service beyond boundaries, and harmony beyond differences.

May such moments continue to inspire society towards compassion, dialogue and the timeless message of Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam — the world is one family — and the Islamic spirit of Insaniyat and Aman (humanity and peace).

Together, we walk the path of understanding, service and global brotherhood. 🌍🤍

#CommunalHarmony | #InterfaithUnity
A delegation comprising 20 distinguished members from the Memon, Kokani, and Jain communities met with Gurudev Shri Shri Ravi Shankar Ji, the renowned spiritual leader and Founder of The Art of Living, on 20th January 2026 at Raheja House Bungalow.
On this meaningful occasion, the All India Memon Jamat Federation conferred upon him its highest honour, “Fakr-e-Insaniyat”, in recognition of his outstanding contributions toward humanity, peace, and communal harmony.

With Allah as our witness, we chose each other.

Detoxification means cleansing the blood. This is done by removing impurities from the blood in the liver, where toxins are processed for elimination. The body also eliminates toxins through the kidneys, intestines, lungs, lymphatic system, and skin.

A detox program can help the body's natural cleansing process by:

1.  Resting the organs through fasting;

2.  Stimulating the liver to drive toxins from the body;

3.  Promoting elimination through the intestines, kidneys, and skin;

4.  Improving circulation of the blood; and

5.  Refueling the body with healthy nutrients.
